channel.title: Short food briefs on yuejn
channel.description: Recent content in Short food briefs on yuejn

item.title: Baking baking soda
item.description: Creating an alkaline agent at home: spread 100 grams of baking soda on parchment paper. Place in oven at 120 degrees Celsius for an hour. The baking soda should reduce by 2/3rds of its weight (so, at 100 grams, should become 66 grams). This is caustic so pls be careful and don’t touch it with your bare hands.

item.title: Roasted aubergine alla Britt
item.description: I took this recipe from Britt who is the kind of cook who thinks everyone knows this stuff already: Pre-heat the oven to 220 degrees Celsius. Cut your aubergine in half. Make criss-cross incisions in each half. Pour lots of oil, getting it into the cuts. Add your spices (paprika, chilli, cumin, tumeric etc) and rub. Roast it, face down, for around 15-20 minutes. During this time, make a paste — I chop and mix red onion, garlic, grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, and tomato paste with some more spices and some salt and pepper.

item.title: Ajitsuke tamago
item.description: Gently peel soft boiled eggs and cover them in a 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp mirin, 3 tbsp water marinade in a refrigerated sealed container overnight.

item.title: Sauce for Cantonese ground beef
item.description: I really like this recipe for Cantonese ground beef rice with eggs, and have started to use the sauce as a base for other things as well: 2 tbsp oyster sauce 2 tbsp Shaoxing wing 2 tbsp light soy sauce 1 tsp dark soy sauce some white pepper

item.title: Sauce for Chinese broccoli
item.description: This is a great sauce we go back to! With thanks to this recipe for crispy tofu and broccoli stir fry. 2 tbsp hoisin sauce 2 tbsp rice vinegar 1 tbsp sesame oil 1 tsp chilli flakes 1 tbsp cornflour 60 ml of water 60 ml light soy sauce
